overview.description: AI-native software development studio building production AI products 10x faster
overview.description_long: QuantumSpring partners with companies under AI pressure to identify high-impact opportunities, ship production AI products, and scale what works — using AI-native engineering with enterprise discipline. Services span AI Opportunity Mapping, AI Product Launchpad, AI Product Evolution, AI Engineering, AI Data Foundation, and Voice AI Agents. Also operates own products including Echota.ai (AI for customer-agent training) and a Voice AI Agents platform built on OpenAI Realtime API.

history.origin_story: Founded in Prague by Ondřej Šťastný (former CDO at mediaport solutions, tech leader at Mall, Allegro, Microsoft, and VMware) with backing from Tomáš Čupr (founder of Rohlik Group). Positions between 'vibe coding' and traditional enterprise teams — lean AI-native squads with senior-only developers.

key_milestones.event: Launched voice AI agents product with <800ms response latency
Published case study showing 90% reduction in time-to-answer for conversational analytics client

notable_people.name: Tomáš Čupr
notable_people.note: Founder of Rohlik Group, Czech serial entrepreneur; investor and advisor

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: QuantumSpring's core value proposition — shipping AI software faster using AI tools like Cursor and Claude Code — is highly susceptible to commoditization as AI agents become more capable of end-to-end software development. No proprietary data moat or patented technology; competitive advantage relies primarily on team expertise and process methodology.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Demand for AI software consulting is strong and growing, with clear PMF among enterprise clients (Rohlik, Pilulka, etc.). However, the market is intensely competitive with many boutique AI studios and large consultancies investing heavily in AI-native delivery. Differentiation on 'lean squad + AI' is real but difficult to defend long-term.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: No known institutional funding. Early-stage startup with a small team, relying on consulting revenue. Tomáš Čupr's backing provides some credibility and network, but the company appears to be bootstrapped or angel-backed at a very early stage with limited runway visibility.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: The combination of high AGI displacement risk (core AI tooling advantage erodes as models improve) and high funding risk (no institutional backing, small team) makes this a high-risk venture. Market risk is partly mitigated by strong Czech tech client base and notable advisor, but the overall profile is high risk for job-seekers seeking stability.

metadata.notes: Very new company (active as of 2026). No CNAIP listing. Tomáš Čupr (Rohlik founder) listed as Investor & Advisor. Products include Echota.ai and Voice AI Agents platform. Primarily Prague-based with EU remote flexibility.
